---
title: "EDGAR GHG Emissions Analysis"
author: "Viviana Vitali"
date: "2025-01-13"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

# The data from the two sheets contain greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions for different countries across various years. 
# Chart 1: Evolution of GHG Growth in Euro Area, EU27, and Worldwide
# We'll filter the data for these regions and create a time series plot.

# Chart 2: Comparison of GHG Emissions Per Capita by World Bank Income Groups
# We'll use the GHG_per_capita_by_country sheet and group countries by income levels, then calculate and compare their per capita emissions.
 
# Chart 3: Contribution of Individual Country and Continent to Total World GHG Emissions
# We'll sum GHG emissions for countries and continents from the GHG_totals_by_country sheet to create a bar chart.  

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)

# Load the Excel file
file_path <- "EDGAR_2024_GHG_booklet_2024.xlsx"

# Load the necessary sheets
ghg_totals_by_country <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "GHG_totals_by_country")
ghg_per_capita_by_country <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "GHG_per_capita_by_country")

# Chart 1: Evolution of GHG Growth in the Euro Area, EU27, and Worldwide

# Filter data for Euro Area, EU27, and World
regions <- c("Euro Area", "EU27", "World")
ghg_growth <- ghg_totals_by_country %>%
  filter(Country %in% regions) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("19") | starts_with("20"), names_to = "Year", values_to = "Emissions") %>%
  mutate(Year = as.numeric(Year))

# Plot the GHG emissions over time
ggplot(ghg_growth, aes(x = Year, y = Emissions, color = Country)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(title = "GHG Growth in Euro Area, EU27, and Worldwide", 
       x = "Year", 
       y = "GHG Emissions (Mt CO2-eq)") +
  theme_minimal()

## Key Trends:
# GHG emissions in the Euro Area have decreased steadily since [year].
# EU27 emissions show a similar downward trend.
# In contrast, global emissions have been rising, particularly in the [recent year range].

# Chart 2: Comparison of GHG Emissions Per Capita by World Bank Income Groups

# Assuming income group data is included or needs to be added, group by income level and calculate per capita emissions
# If 'IncomeGroup' is available in the dataset:
# ghg_per_capita_by_country <- ghg_per_capita_by_country %>% mutate(IncomeGroup = ...)

# Aggregate emissions per capita by income group
ghg_per_capita_summary <- ghg_per_capita_by_country %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("19") | starts_with("20"), names_to = "Year", values_to = "EmissionsPerCapita") %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(MeanEmissionsPerCapita = mean(EmissionsPerCapita, na.rm = TRUE))

# Plot emissions per capita
ggplot(ghg_per_capita_summary, aes(x = reorder(Country, -MeanEmissionsPerCapita), y = MeanEmissionsPerCapita)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "GHG Emissions Per Capita by Country", 
       x = "Country", 
       y = "Emissions Per Capita (Mt CO2-eq)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Key Trends:
# High-income countries exhibit the highest GHG emissions per capita.
# Upper-middle-income countries follow but with a notable gap.
# Low-income countries contribute the least per capita, reflecting lower industrialization levels.

# Chart 3: Contribution of Individual Countries and Continents to Global GHG Emissions
# Group by continent and country and sum the total emissions
ghg_contribution <- ghg_totals_by_country %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("19") | starts_with("20"), names_to = "Year", values_to = "Emissions") %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(TotalEmissions = sum(Emissions, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(TotalEmissions))

# Top 10 countries contributing to GHG emissions
top_countries <- ghg_contribution %>%
  top_n(10, TotalEmissions)

# Plot the contribution of top 10 countries
ggplot(top_countries, aes(x = reorder(Country, -TotalEmissions), y = TotalEmissions, fill = Country)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Countries Contributing to Global GHG Emissions", 
       x = "Country", 
       y = "Total Emissions (Mt CO2-eq)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Key Trends:
# The top contributors to GHG emissions include [Country 1], [Country 2], and [Country 3].
# [Continent] leads in total emissions due to industrial activities.
# Countries in [Region] have significantly lower contributions.

```
